findings.description: The North Carolina state seal image at the top of the letter is missing in the HTML rendering.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a North Carolina state seal/coat of arms at the top center. The HTML rendering omits this image.

findings.description: The letterhead layout with official names on the left and seal in the center is not preserved; the HTML renders them as a simpler stacked layout.
findings.evidence: PDF has ROY COOPER/Governor, MICHAEL S. REGAN/Secretary, S. Daniel Smith/Director on the left with the seal centered. HTML renders these as a header block without the seal.

findings.description: The underlined formatting of 'Certified Mail' and 'Return Receipt Requested' is preserved in the HTML version.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML show underlined text for these elements - this is correct.

findings.description: The DEQ logo at the bottom of page 1 is rendered differently - the HTML shows it as a horizontal bar/footer rather than the logo image.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the DEQ logo with accompanying address text. HTML renders the address information as a footer bar without the logo graphic.

findings.description: The handwritten signature of Judith A. Wehner is missing in the HTML rendering.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 shows a handwritten signature above 'Judith A. Wehner'. HTML shows only the typed name without the signature image.

findings.description: The 'Certified Mail' text on page 2 has underline formatting in the HTML that matches the PDF.
findings.evidence: Both versions show underlined 'Certified Mail' text.

findings.description: No h1 heading exists in the HTML document. The document lacks any heading hierarchy whatsoever - there are no h1, h2, h3, or any heading tags used to structure the content.
findings.evidence:
The HTML uses only

,

    ,
  1. , , , and tags for content but no heading elements. A proper conversion should have at least an h1 for the document title or subject.

findings.description: The HTML contains nested <main> elements. The outer <main> wraps the entire body content, and then three additional <main> elements are used inside it for page content sections. This creates invalid HTML structure, though it does not duplicate content.
findings.evidence: The outermost <main> in <body> contains additional <main> elements for page 1 content, page 1 footer area, and page 2 content.

findings.description: Alt text for images is generic rather than descriptive. The images are scans of the full letter pages but the alt text simply says 'Scan of page 1' and 'Scan of page 2' rather than describing the content (e.g., the NC state seal on page 1).
findings.evidence: alt="Scan of page 1" and alt="Scan of page 2"
